name: Moldbug Sold Out
abstract: This article critically examines Curtis Yarvin, also known as Mencius Moldbug, arguing that he has "sold out" by abandoning the core tenets of his original neoreactionary philosophy. It contrasts his earlier, intricate theories of "neocameralism"—which proposed a shareholder-controlled, cryptographically-secured autocracy designed to avert the pitfalls of democracy, populism, and traditional totalitarianism through mechanisms like a removable CEO-like dictator and strict passivism—with his post-2015 support for the MAGA movement. The author contends that Yarvin's current endorsements directly contradict the very safeguards and warnings he meticulously outlined against the dangers of populist, democratic-rooted movements, implying a compromise of principles for perceived political expediency.
